cases.byzantine: excessively complicated, especially in a way that is hard to follow
cases.twilight years: the final period of a person's life, old age
cases.nacre: the iridescent substance forming the inner layer of a mollusk shell, mother-of-pearl
cases.rote: mechanical repetition used as a way of learning, without deep understanding
cases.exhilarating: making someone feel very happy, animated, or elated
cases.mausoleum: a large, grand tomb, especially a stately building
cases.abyssal: relating to the deepest, darkest parts, immeasurably deep
cases.abject: experienced or present to the maximum degree, utterly hopeless or humiliating
cases.vindicate: to clear someone of blame or suspicion, to prove someone right
cases.cavalier: showing arrogant or offhand disregard, dismissive
cases.insinuate: to suggest something unpleasant in an indirect way
cases.vertigo: a sensation of dizziness and spinning, especially from heights
cases.battle of attrition: a conflict where each side wears the other down gradually
cases.lucidity: the quality of being clear and easily understood, mental clarity
cases.catatonic: in a state of unresponsiveness or immobility
cases.peruse: to read or examine something thoroughly
cases.siphon: to draw off or extract something gradually, often illicitly
cases.deterrence: the act of discouraging an action through fear of consequences
cases.machination: a plot or scheme, especially a devious one
cases.killjoy: a person who spoils the enjoyment of others
cases.eclectic: deriving ideas or style from a diverse range of sources
cases.squall: a sudden violent gust of wind or storm
cases.benevolence: the quality of being kind and well-meaning
cases.voracious: having a very eager approach to an activity, insatiable
cases.decorum: behavior in keeping with good taste and propriety
cases.venerable: accorded great respect, especially because of age or wisdom
cases.epitaph: a phrase written in memory of a dead person, often on a tombstone
cases.whetstone: a stone used for sharpening tools, or something that hones a quality
cases.willful: stubbornly determined to have one's own way
cases.machiavellian: cunning and unscrupulous in the pursuit of power
cases.pantheon: all the gods of a people collectively, or a group of highly esteemed people
cases.dally: to waste time, act in a leisurely way
cases.coddle: to treat someone in an indulgent or overprotective way
cases.devious: showing skill in achieving one's ends by underhand means
cases.paramour: a lover, especially the illicit partner of a married person
cases.salacious: having an excessive interest in sexual matters
cases.paper-pusher: someone with a dull administrative or bureaucratic job
cases.triumvirate: a group of three powerful or notable people or things
cases.despoil: to steal or damage valuable things from a place, to plunder
cases.mettle: a person's ability to cope well with difficulties, spirit and resilience
cases.hegemon: a leader or dominant state with control over others
cases.casus belli: an act used to justify starting a war
cases.prodigious: remarkably great in extent or degree
cases.philanderer: a man who has many casual romantic affairs
cases.schadenfreude: pleasure derived from another person's misfortune
cases.ascetic: characterized by severe self-discipline and abstinence from indulgence
cases.sequester: to isolate or hide something away
cases.apogee: the highest point of something, the culmination
cases.deluge: a severe flood, or an overwhelming amount of something
cases.nascent: just coming into existence, beginning to develop
cases.adroit: clever or skillful, especially in a physical or difficult situation
cases.beyond reproach: perfect, so good that no criticism is possible
cases.haruspex: a person in ancient times who practiced divination by examining entrails
cases.burgeoning: beginning to grow or increase rapidly
cases.utilitarian: designed to be useful and practical rather than attractive
cases.veiled threat: a warning that is expressed indirectly rather than stated openly
cases.enrapture: to fill someone with intense delight
cases.supple: bending and moving easily and gracefully, flexible
cases.slight: to insult someone by treating them without proper respect
cases.low blow: an unfair or unkind action or remark
cases.insurgency: an active revolt or rebellion against an authority
cases.indoctrinate: to teach someone to accept a set of beliefs uncritically
cases.highwayman: a robber who held up travelers, historically on horseback
